summary: 2026-06-21 11:31:54 PDT — The branch preserves identifiers, routes, schemas, and application math, but it does not yet satisfy the documentation and framing specs. The full test/build gate could not run because the read-only sandbox blocks required temporary writes and sockets.

findings.title: Privacy policy falsely says the server handles overlay snapshots
findings.claim: Under “How our server handles your data,” the policy lists the live aggregate overlay snapshot as a second server-handled data flow. The application server has no overlay route; `buildOverlayState()` is sent through Zoom’s panel-to-camera message bridge. This repeats the inaccurate architecture the second story was intended to correct.
findings.suggestion: State that the server handles only saved configuration. Describe the aggregate overlay snapshot separately as an in-client Zoom camera-context message that the application server neither receives nor retains.

findings.title: Privacy policy omits half of the canonical definition
findings.claim: The privacy page says opportunity cost is the value of the “best work” and explains that pay is private, but never states “highest and best work” or the required second reason: pay understates meeting cost. AC3 explicitly requires both reasons in this page.
findings.suggestion: Use the canonical definition here, including “highest and best work” and both the privacy and understatement reasons.

findings.title: Public documentation misstates the per-participant math
findings.claim: This page says the app multiplies the per-person estimates by elapsed time and participant count. The per-participant model instead sums each participant’s resolved value and prorates that sum by elapsed time; count multiplication belongs only to the simple average model. Similar wording appears in `docs/privacy.html` and `docs/terms.html`.
findings.suggestion: Describe the two models accurately: sum resolved per-person values and apply elapsed time, or use attendee count × average value in simple mode.

findings.title: Compensation-era semantic comments remain
findings.claim: The branch’s comment-only reframe leaves this core matching module defining values as “hourly rate” and describing a “loaded-cost multiplier” at lines 12 and 72. That conflicts with the required opportunity-cost framing across comments, despite the application logic remaining unchanged.
findings.suggestion: Update these comments—and the “loaded-cost overhead” comment in `matching.test.js`—to opportunity-cost/value-neutral wording only.

findings.title: README duplicates the canonical definition
findings.claim: The spec requires a one-line definition plus a link, with `dev-docs/opportunity-cost-rate.md` as the single source of truth. The added block restates the full definition and both reasons across six lines, creating the duplication the hybrid decision explicitly avoids.
findings.suggestion: Collapse this to one line defining rate as hourly opportunity cost, not pay, followed by the canonical-note link.
